pr_summary: This PR adds order cancellation and status polling functionality to the order service.
verdict_reason: The verdict was chosen because the PR contains critical findings, including an infinite polling loop in the pollOrderStatus function and missing null checks for order objects.

findings.title: Authorization Issue
findings.description: The function bulkCancelOrders cancels orders without checking if the 'system' user has permission to cancel the orders.
findings.suggestion: Add a permission check before cancelling orders, e.g., if (request.user_id === order.user_id || request.user_role === 'admin') {

findings.title: Authorization Issue
findings.description: The function cancelOrder cancels an order without checking if the user has permission to cancel the order.
findings.suggestion: Add a permission check before cancelling orders, e.g., if (request.user_id === order.user_id || request.user_role === 'admin') {

findings.title: Sensitive Data Exposure
findings.description: The function cancelOrder sends an email to the user with their email address, potentially exposing sensitive data.
findings.suggestion: Use a secure method to send notifications, e.g., use a notification service that doesn't expose user email addresses.

findings.title: Infinite Polling
findings.description: The pollOrderStatus function contains an infinite loop with no visible timeout or retry limit, which can cause unbounded resource consumption.
findings.suggestion: Add a retry limit or timeout to the loop, e.g., `let retries = 0; while (status === 'pending' && retries < 10) { ...; retries++; }`

findings.title: Missing Null Check
findings.description: The order object is not checked for null before accessing its properties.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check before accessing order properties: if (!order) { throw new Error('Order not found'); }

findings.title: Missing Null Check
findings.description: The order object is not checked for null before accessing its properties.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check before accessing order properties: if (!order) { throw new Error('Order not found'); }

findings.title: Invalid Assumption About Data
findings.description: The discounts object may not contain the given discount code, resulting in NaN.
findings.suggestion: Add a check for the discount code: if (!(discountCode in discounts)) { throw new Error('Invalid discount code'); }

findings.line_content: const discounts: any = { SAVE10: 0.1, SAVE20: 0.2, SAVE50: 0.5 };
findings.title: Poor Typing
findings.description: The discounts object is typed as 'any', which can harm maintainability. It would be better to define a specific type for the discounts object.
findings.suggestion: Define a specific type for the discounts object, for example: interface Discount { [code: string]: number; } const discounts: Discount = { SAVE10: 0.1, SAVE20: 0.2, SAVE50: 0.5 };

positive_observations: The PR adds a calculateDiscount function to handle discounts based on discount codes.
The PR introduces a bulkCancelOrders function to cancel multiple orders at once.
missing_tests: Test the cancelOrder function when the order does not exist. This matters because orderRepo.findById(orderId) may return null. The expected behavior is that the request returns an error response instead of crashing.

Test the pollOrderStatus function when the status never changes from pending. This matters because the polling loop has no visible timeout. The expected behavior is that the operation eventually times out instead of running indefinitely.

Test the bulkCancelOrders function when the orderIds array is empty. This matters because the function does not check for an empty array. The expected behavior is that the function completes without errors.

Test the calculateDiscount function when the discountCode is not found in the discounts object. This matters because the function does not handle unknown discount codes. The expected behavior is that the function returns the original price.

Test the cancelOrder function when the userId is null or undefined. This matters because the function does not check for null or undefined userId. The expected behavior is that the request returns an error response instead of crashing.

Test the pollOrderStatus function when the orderRepo.findById(orderId) returns null. This matters because the function does not handle the case where the order is not found. The expected behavior is that the function returns an error or a default status.

Test the calculateDiscount function when the price is negative. This matters because the function does not handle negative prices. The expected behavior is that the function returns an error or a default value.
